observations.summary: Attempt 2 (a2) was launched while attempt 1 (a1) was still mid-playbook, causing two runs to modify the same files concurrently; a2 never completed (no story_end, last event is step_start for review-nfrs at index 10)
observations.evidence: Journal story_start for a1 at 2026-07-13T11:53:16 (attempt 1); journal story_start for a2 at 2026-07-13T12:59:46 (attempt 2) while a1 was still in implement-story (a1 implement-story step_end not until 13:10:51). Journal shows interleaved step events: a2 unit-tests (step 6) ended 14:16:35 marking 10 E2E tests as expected-to-fail, then a1 e2e-tests (step 7) ended 15:45:42 finding only 3 of those markers remaining, then a1 review-tests (step 9) removed the 3, then a2 review-tests (step 9) found 0 — the runs interfered on the same test file. grep '5.5-a2' journal.jsonl | grep story_end returns NO STORY_END FOR A2. a2's last recorded event is step_start for review-nfrs (index 10) at 16:24:06; steps 11-12 and story_end are absent. a1 completed all 12 steps and committed (465ea50) at 16:28:16.

observations.summary: Both e2e-tests steps (a1 and a2) timed out with rc:124 and salvaged output classified as success — 6th and 7th occurrence across stories (2.3, 3.9, 3.11, 5.1, 5.3, 5.5x2)
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 6: {"ts":"2026-07-13T14:45:43Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"E2E tests (5.5) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes"}. runner-errors.jsonl line 7: {"ts":"2026-07-13T15:46:36Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"E2E tests (5.5) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes"}. Journal step_end for a2 e2e-tests: status success, durationMs 5402246, responseExcerpt is mid-stream narration ('Now let me run the `on_complete` step from the workflow customization.') — not a completion summary. Journal step_end for a1 e2e-tests: status success, durationMs 8999505 (max for this step), responseExcerpt starts with 'Let me verify the checklist:' — also mid-stream narration. Both steps were classified as COMPLETE despite salvaged output. Prior occurrences: ledger entries 41 (3.9), 56 (3.11), 70 (5.1), 86 (5.2 non-recurrence), 90 (5.3), 97 (5.3), 110 (5.4 non-recurrence).

observations.summary: 3 halts across story 5.5, all phantom: a1 e2e-tests halt was timeout-triggered (confirms 5.3 validate-story pattern); a1 and a2 create-story halts had no runner-error and no question in the responseExcerpt (same class as the 5.3 e2e-tests hypothesized halt, still unconfirmable without traces)
observations.evidence: a1 e2e-tests: halts:1, outcomeHistory ['COMPLETE'], responseExcerpt 'Let me verify the checklist:' (mid-stream narration, no question), runner-errors line 6 rc:124 — CONFIRMED timeout-triggered phantom halt, same as ledger entry 89 (5.3 validate-story). a1 create-story: halts:1, outcomeHistory ['COMPLETE'], responseExcerpt 'Confirmed — Story 5.5 stays as-is...' (completion summary, no question), no runner-error, duration 650432ms (within normal range) — matches 5.3 e2e-tests hypothesized pattern (ledger entry 91). a2 create-story: halts:1, outcomeHistory ['COMPLETE'], responseExcerpt 'Understood — keeping the existing story as-is. No changes made. Bye Marius! 👋' (completion summary, no question), no runner-error, duration 1782599ms (elevated but not extreme) — same pattern. Trends haltsPerStory: 5.5:3. No traces exist for this run (ls _bmad-output/pipeline/traces/ returns NO TRACES DIR). Prior hypothesized observation (ledger entries 91, 103) has nextStep pointing to traces/5.3-a1-20260712T095855/e2e-tests.json — trace-view reports no trace, traces directory does not exist. The 5.5 create-story halts provide new data points for the non-timeout phantom halt hypothesis but cannot confirm the cause without traces.
observations.hypothesis: The a1 and a2 create-story phantom halts were triggered by classifier ambiguity — the response was neither clearly COMPLETE nor QUESTION at some mid-stream point, causing the halt form to fire spuriously. The a1 e2e-tests halt was timeout-triggered (confirmed). The non-timeout create-story halts match the 5.3 e2e-tests pattern but the cause remains unconfirmable without traces.
observations.nextStep: When trace recording is enabled, widen into traces/5.5-a2-20260713T125945/create-story.json with --grep 'HALT' or --grep 'QUESTION' to find the mid-stream point where the halt form fired; check whether a genuine question was posed or whether the halt was triggered by classifier ambiguity. Cross-reference with traces/5.5-a1-20260713T115316/create-story.json for the same step.

Chunk 2 (tests) and chunk 3 (other changes) noted for follow-up.' Journal step_end for a1 review-code: 'Review Summary — Story 5.5 (Chunk 1: Production Code)' — 8 patches, 7 deferred, 5 dismissed. Journal step_end for a2 review-code: 'Chunk: 1 of 3 (production code, 983 lines)' — 8 patches, 1 deferred, 12 dismissed. Playbook review-code prompt: 'Chunk automatically if over threshold (take first chunk, note rest for follow-up) — do not wait for confirmation.' The agent followed the prompt (noted remaining chunks) but no subsequent step in the playbook reviews the remaining chunks. Trends show review-code-chunks-remaining-unreviewed: distinctRuns 1 (prior occurrence). This is the 2nd occurrence.

observations.summary: Prior confirmed observation (ledger entry 57, Story 3.11; ledger entries 78, 87, 98, 111 — Stories 5.1 through 5.4 deduced non-recurrence) — the pattern did NOT recur in 5.5: E2E tests ran (a1: 8 passed, 3 expected-to-fail; a2: timed out), indicating the auth.setup.ts seed-user issue remains resolved; the 3 expected-to-fail tests are due to test-infrastructure timing issues, not global env config
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for a1 e2e-tests: '8 passed, 3 skipped (test.fixme with documented reasons).' The 3 expected-to-fail tests are due to 'Dev server compilation timing (20-30s)', 'setMessages/Playwright assertion timing' — test-infrastructure issues, not the global env config issue that blocked ALL E2E test execution in 3.11. Compare to ledger entry 57 (Story 3.11): 'no .env.local exists with DATABASE_URL/AUTH_SECRET/CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEK — this blocks ALL E2E test execution.' 8 tests passing indicates the global env issue is resolved.

amendments.prompt: Review the story implementation. Spec file: {implementation_artifacts}/`<epic>-<story>-*.md` (Tier 1); diff baseline = its frontmatter `baseline_commit`, or all uncommitted changes if absent — resolve without asking. Chunk automatically if over threshold for internal organization only — review every chunk sequentially before finishing, starting from the first. Do not wait for confirmation between chunks. Skip the pre-review checkpoint. For decision-needed findings, consult @_bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide and continue (if deferring, write a one-line reason instead of asking). HALT only for decisions no rule covers. After triage, choose 'apply every patch' (no per-finding confirmation) and end — no next-steps menu.
amendments.rationale: review-code reviews only chunk 1 of multi-chunk diffs and notes the rest for follow-up, but no follow-up step exists in the playbook — chunks 2 and 3 go permanently unreviewed; changing the prompt to review all chunks sequentially eliminates the gap without adding a step.
